story.iWant: number sense questions that test my understanding of quantities and comparisons
story.soThat: the app can identify if I struggle with basic number comprehension

tasks.task: Implement dot rendering with SVG circles (random non-overlapping positions)
Implement number line with click handler calculating percentage position
Add randomization for dot patterns and target numbers per session
Record timing with performance.now() for timeToAnswer
Create QuestionCard wrapper component for consistent styling
Write unit tests achieving 100% coverage for both components

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Quantity Comparison shows two groups of dots (5-20 dots each)
QC asks "Which group has more dots?" with Left/Right/Same options
NL asks "Where is [target number] on this line?"
NL user taps/clicks position, ±10% tolerance considered correct
NL records userAnswer (position), correctAnswer, error (absolute difference), timeToAnswer
All questions rendered in QuestionCard component with consistent styling
No visual feedback during assessment (no reveal of correct answer)
Questions randomize: Dot patterns and target numbers vary per session

doc.title: Product Requirements
doc.snippet: 10-question assessment that identifies user's math and spatial weaknesses including number sense and quantity comparison abilities

doc.title: Architecture Spec
doc.snippet: Assessment uses features/assessment/, routes/AssessmentRoute.tsx, assessments table (Dexie), Multi-step wizard with React Hook Form

doc.title: UX Design
doc.snippet: Mobile-first with generous padding and 44px+ tap targets. Warm, calm visual design avoiding anxiety-inducing elements

doc.title: Epic 2 Tech Spec
doc.snippet: QuantityComparison for dot counting, NumberLineEstimation for number placement. Questions 1-3 are number sense domain

file.reason: Parent wizard shell that will render question components - integrate QuantityComparison and NumberLineEstimation here
Feature module entry point - export new question components from here
Session state management - may need to store question results
Reuse for answer selection buttons in QuantityComparison
May use as base for QuestionCard styling
Custom render with providers for component tests

package.name: framer-motion
package.reason: Smooth animations for dot rendering and number line interactions

package.name: lucide-react
package.reason: Icons if needed for visual feedback

constraints.constraint: 100% test coverage required for all new code
Mobile-first responsive design with 44px+ tap targets
Performance: less than 100ms input latency, 60fps interactions
Use performance.now() for accurate timing measurements
Dot patterns must be random and non-overlapping
Number line tolerance is ±10% for correct answer

interface.name: QuestionResult
interface.signature: { questionId: string; domain: 'number_sense' | 'spatial' | 'operations'; questionType: string; correct: boolean; responseTime: number; userAnswer: string | number; expectedAnswer: string | number; difficulty: 1 | 2 | 3; }

interface.name: QuantityComparisonProps
interface.signature: { leftCount: number; rightCount: number; onAnswer: (answer: 'left' | 'right' | 'same') => void; }

interface.name: NumberLineProps
interface.signature: { range: [number, number]; targetNumber: number; onAnswer: (position: number) => void; }

interface.name: AssessmentWizard.handleDemoAnswer
interface.signature: Replace demo answer handler with actual question integration

tests.standards: Testing follows architecture.md ADR-005: Vitest for unit/integration tests, React Testing Library for component tests. AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert). Co-located test files (*).test.tsx. 100% coverage enforced by CI. Use custom render from tests/test-utils.tsx with all providers wrapped.

ideas.idea: Test that QuantityComparison renders correct number of dots in each group (5-20 range)
Test that answer buttons (Left/Right/Same) are rendered and clickable
Test that onAnswer callback receives correct parameters including timing
Test NumberLine renders with correct range labels (0 and max)
Test NumberLine displays target number prompt
Test click handler calculates correct position percentage
Test that all required fields are recorded in answer callback
Test QuestionCard wrapper provides consistent styling
Test randomization produces different patterns on multiple renders
